title: Review pipeline refactor: synthesis as orchestrator + reviewer prompt overhaul

items.title: Harden TLDR daemon startup for every workspace
items.description: Audit workspace-manager.ts:825 and agents.ts:1619 where TLDR daemon start is supposed to happen. Make it an explicit, idempotent ensureTldrDaemon() helper: start the daemon, poll for readiness (up to 10 s), and if it never comes up emit a structured warning to the console AND to the dashboard event stream — not a silent no-op. Add tldrStartedAt timestamp to agent state. Confirm postMergeLifecycle tldrService.warm(true) still fires.
acceptanceCriteria: Every workspace-setup and agent-spawn path calls a single ensureTldrDaemon() helper that is idempotent
If daemon fails to start within 10 s, a structured warning appears in console AND dashboard event stream
tldrStartedAt persisted to agent state on success
postMergeLifecycle warm() still fires after merge
No silent fallback — failure is always surfaced

items.title: Expose TLDR daemon status in pan doctor and dashboard resources tree
items.description: pan doctor should check TLDR daemon per active workspace. Dashboard resources tree (alongside Docker containers and Traefik) should show each workspace's TLDR daemon with a status chip (running/stopped/error) and a restart action. Use the same health-polling pattern as Docker container status.
acceptanceCriteria: pan doctor output includes TLDR daemon status for each active workspace
Dashboard resources tree shows TLDR daemon entry per workspace with running/stopped/error chip
Restart action triggers ensureTldrDaemon() for that workspace
Status updates in real-time without page refresh

items.title: Wire TLDR metrics into the issue-level cost pipeline
items.description: captureTldrMetrics() exists in tldr-daemon.ts but is only called in tests. Wire it into the production cost-event flush path. Add tldrInterceptions and tldrTokensSaved to IssueStats aggregation. Surface them in the dashboard per-issue cost view. Add tldrMisses (files where TLDR was tried but daemon was down). Raw data already exists in ~/.panopticon/tldr/<hash>/interceptions.log.
acceptanceCriteria: captureTldrMetrics() called before each cost-event flush in production
tldrInterceptions and tldrTokensSaved fields present in IssueStats
Per-issue cost view shows tldrInterceptions and tldrTokensSaved
tldrMisses tracked and surfaced when daemon is down
SQLite migration adds new fields to cost_events table

items.title: Build review context manifest module
items.description: New module src/lib/cloister/review-context.ts. Exports buildReviewContext(issueId, opts): Promise<ReviewContext>. Produces .pan/review/<runId>/context.json with: issueId, runId (ulid), baseSha, headSha, diffStats, acceptanceCriteria from vBRIEF, policyNotes from .claude/rules/*.md filenames, and a files[] array. Each file: path, status, size, contentHash, kind (source/test/config/generated/lock/doc), riskRank (1=highest...5=lowest), tldrAvailable, tldrSummary (embedded ~800-token output). Files[] sorted by riskRank. Total manifest token budget: 30k — truncate per-file TLDR at 1.2k.
acceptanceCriteria: buildReviewContext() produces valid context.json at .pan/review/<runId>/context.json
files[] sorted by riskRank ascending (highest risk first)
tldrSummary embedded for rank 1-2 files when daemon is up
tldrAvailable: false when daemon is down — graceful degrade, no crash
Total manifest stays under 30k tokens (truncate per-file TLDR at 1.2k)
policyNotes populated from .claude/rules/*.md file names in repo
acceptanceCriteria copied from spec.vbrief.json for the issue
Unit tests cover: daemon-up, daemon-down, >30-file diff, budget truncation

items.title: Extend spawnRun to support review sub-roles
items.description: spawnRun(issueId, role, opts) needs to accept opts.subRole for the review role ('security'|'correctness'|'performance'|'requirements'). When subRole is set: resolve model via resolveModel('review', subRole) from config; use the matching .claude/agents/code-review-<subRole>.md agent definition; inject context manifest path and assigned output file path (.pan/review/<runId>/<subRole>.md) into spawn prompt. Sub-reviewer sessions named review-<issueId>-<subRole>.
acceptanceCriteria: spawnRun(id, 'review', { subRole: 'security' }) spawns a session named review-<id>-security
Model resolved via resolveModel('review', 'security') from config — not hard-coded
Context manifest path injected into spawn prompt as ## Review Context block
Output file path injected into spawn prompt as **Output file** line
All four sub-roles spawn successfully in integration test
spawnRun without subRole still spawns the synthesis (top-level review) role

items.title: Build reviewer output-watch and stall-detection library
items.description: New module src/lib/cloister/review-monitor.ts. Exports waitForReviewerOutputs(runId, subRoles, opts): AsyncGenerator yielding completion events as each output file appears. Stall policy (configurable): waitWindowMs=8min, retryOnce=true, proceedAfterMs=waitWindowMs*2. On stall: retry once (re-deliver spawn prompt). On second stall or proceedAfter: emit { subRole, status: 'stalled' } and continue. 2+ stalls: emit { overallStatus: 'failed' }. Pure TypeScript — synthesis prompt stays declarative.
acceptanceCriteria: waitForReviewerOutputs yields { subRole, status: 'completed', filePath } on file appearance
Stall detected when file doesn't appear within waitWindowMs
Retry fires once — re-delivers spawn prompt to stalled session
proceedAfter timeout emits stalled event and continues with completed reviewers
2+ stalls emits overallStatus: 'failed'
Unit tests: all complete, one stall+retry success, one stall+timeout, two stalls
waitWindowMs and retryOnce configurable via opts

items.title: Ensure synthesis death reaps sub-reviewer sessions
items.description: When synthesis session is killed, the four sub-reviewers must also die. Implement via process-group ownership (synthesis spawns sub-reviewers in same process group) plus a Stop-hook backstop that sends tmux kill-session to each review-<issueId>-<subRole> session on synthesis exit. Integration test: kill synthesis, verify all four sub-reviewers gone within 5 s.
acceptanceCriteria: Killing synthesis tmux session reaps all four sub-reviewer sessions within 5 s
Reap works via process-group OR explicit stop hook — at least one verified
Sub-reviewer sessions named review-<issueId>-<subRole> for deterministic kill targeting
Integration test: kill synthesis, poll tmux list-sessions, confirm all four gone
Reaper does not error if a sub-reviewer already exited naturally

items.title: Delete bash coordinator; wire pan review request to spawnRun
items.description: Delete: spawnReviewCoordinatorSession(), runReviewerRound(), waitForReviewerSessions(), selectCompletedReviewers(), and the bash launcher template wrapping pan review run. Wire pan review request <id> directly to spawnRun(issueId, 'review'). Issue goes directly to in-review state when synthesis spawns. Delete the EXIT-trap zombie fix from PAN-977 — the session-reaper bead replaces it.
acceptanceCriteria: spawnReviewCoordinatorSession, runReviewerRound, waitForReviewerSessions, selectCompletedReviewers deleted — no references remain
pan review request <id> successfully spawns a synthesis session via spawnRun
No bash launcher orchestration remains for the review path
Issue transitions directly to in-review state when synthesis spawns
npm run typecheck passes after deletion
Existing review-agent.test.ts updated to cover new direct-spawn path

items.title: Write roles/review.md — synthesis as LLM orchestrator
items.description: Rewrite roles/review.md following PRD §5 and §6 principles. Sections: (1) Pre-read context manifest; note TLDR coverage gaps. (2) Spawn four sub-roles via spawnRun in parallel. (3) Monitor via stall/retry/proceed policy (8 min wait, retry-once, proceed after 16 min). (4) Verdict: !/⊗ always blocks; ~ on hot path blocks; ? never blocks. (5) Write synthesis.md (# Verdict: first line) + synthesis.json. (6) Deliver: CHANGES_REQUESTED triggers deliverAgentMessage; APPROVED transitions to test role. One completion signal: both files exist. No sentinels. No curl. No anxiety language. Target ≤ 200 lines.
acceptanceCriteria: roles/review.md ≤ 200 lines
No banned phrases: 'read-only reviewer', 'MUST complete 3 review passes', 'your turn does not end', 'Read each changed file', 'non-negotiable'
Spawn section shows four parallel spawnRun calls with correct sub-role names
Stall policy documented: 8 min wait, retry-once, proceed-without after 16 min
Severity policy table present: !/⊗ always blocks; ~ on hot path blocks; ? never blocks
One completion signal: synthesis.md + synthesis.json written

items.title: Write .claude/agents/code-review-security.md — lean sub-reviewer
items.description: Rewrite security sub-reviewer following PRD §6 P1-P5, P7, P8. One write surface (output file only). TLDR-first reads — consult tldrSummary from manifest, use Read only when TLDR insufficient to confirm a finding at a specific line. Scope: security vulnerabilities introduced by this PR only; pre-existing unchanged-file issues are ? max. Coverage statement required in output. One Write at end. Exit line: 'Security review complete — N blockers, M highs, K nits'. Target ≤ 120 lines.
acceptanceCriteria: File ≤ 120 lines
No banned phrases
TLDR-first read instruction present
Scope boundary: changed files only; unchanged = ? severity max
Coverage statement required in output
One Write at end — no mid-flight writes
tools frontmatter: Read, Grep, Glob, Bash, Write

items.title: Write .claude/agents/code-review-correctness.md — bugs only, no AC overlap
items.description: Rewrite correctness sub-reviewer. Remove ALL AC checks, vBRIEF spec checks, and bead scans — those belong exclusively to requirements reviewer. Correctness focus: bugs introduced by changed code (null dereferences, off-by-ones, type mismatches, race conditions, unhandled rejections, TDZ violations). Explicitly state: 'Do not check acceptance criteria — that is requirements reviewer scope.' TLDR-first reads. One Write at end. Target ≤ 120 lines.
acceptanceCriteria: File ≤ 120 lines
No AC checking, no vBRIEF spec checks, no bead scanning — confirmed absent
Explicit line: 'Do not check acceptance criteria — that is requirements reviewer scope'
TLDR-first reads, one Write at end
No banned phrases

items.title: Write .claude/agents/code-review-performance.md — hot paths only
items.description: Rewrite performance sub-reviewer. Perf regressions on hot paths in changed code only. Non-hot-path issues are ? (nit), never blocker. Hot-path definition stated: request handlers, DB query paths, WS message dispatch, tight render loops. Highlight tldr_impact for hot-path discovery. TLDR-first. One Write at end. Target ≤ 120 lines.
acceptanceCriteria: File ≤ 120 lines
Hot-path definition stated explicitly
Non-hot-path perf = ? severity max — stated explicitly
tldr_impact highlighted as key tool for hot-path discovery
TLDR-first, one Write at end, no banned phrases

items.title: Write .claude/agents/code-review-requirements.md — sole owner of AC traceability
items.description: Rewrite requirements sub-reviewer. Sole reviewer checking AC coverage and bead alignment. Reads acceptanceCriteria from context manifest (pre-populated by synthesis). For each AC: find the specific code that implements it with file:line evidence. 'Architecture in place but not wired' = FAIL. Checks .beads/issues.jsonl for closed beads — verifies described changes appear in diff. AC-by-AC PASS/FAIL table required in output. TLDR-first. Target ≤ 140 lines.
acceptanceCriteria: File ≤ 140 lines
Reads acceptanceCriteria from context manifest (not vBRIEF file directly)
Output includes AC-by-AC PASS/FAIL table with file:line evidence for each PASS
Bead alignment check: closed beads' described changes present in diff
'Wired' rule stated: architecture in place but not called = FAIL
TLDR-first reads, one Write at end, no banned phrases

items.title: Delete legacy review prompt files and update all references
items.description: Delete: agents/pan-review-agent.md, src/lib/cloister/prompts/review.md, and all five src/lib/cloister/prompts/review/*.prompt-template.md files. Grep-sweep all references before deleting. Update loaders, skill files, plan.md references. Check and update or delete docs/REVIEW-AGENT-ARCHITECTURE.md if now stale. npm run typecheck must pass after deletions.
acceptanceCriteria: All seven legacy files deleted
grep for 'pan-review-agent' and 'code-review-synthesis.prompt-template' returns no source hits
npm run typecheck passes after deletions
npm run lint passes after deletions
docs/REVIEW-AGENT-ARCHITECTURE.md updated or removed

items.title: Wire synthesis verdict to PR comment and work-agent feedback
items.description: After synthesis writes synthesis.md + synthesis.json: (1) post GitHub PR review comment via existing postGithubReview() path (rename/move from review-agent.ts to work under new role structure); (2) on CHANGES_REQUESTED, deliver feedback via deliverAgentMessage(). synthesis.md tail markers (REVIEW_RESULT, NOTES, FILES_REVIEWED, SECURITY_ISSUES, PERFORMANCE_ISSUES) must still be present — work-agent parser keys on them. Cloister state machine reads synthesis.json verdict to transition issue: approved → test role, changes_requested → back to work.
acceptanceCriteria: synthesis.md tail markers present and parseable by existing work-agent feedback reader
GitHub PR review comment posted with correct APPROVE/REQUEST_CHANGES event
On CHANGES_REQUESTED: deliverAgentMessage() delivers synthesis.md content to work agent
On APPROVED: issue transitions to test role
synthesis.json verdict field drives state transition — no text parsing
Integration test: mock synthesis.json with each verdict, verify correct state transition

items.title: Integration tests: convoy orchestration, stall handling, reap, verdict
items.description: New test file tests/lib/cloister/review-pipeline.integration.test.ts covering: (1) Happy path — all four sub-reviewers complete, synthesis emits approved verdict. (2) One stall + retry succeeds — verdict uses all 4. (3) One stall + timeout — synthesis proceeds with 3, Summary notes omission. (4) Two stalls — verdict is failed. (5) Synthesis kill reaps all 4 sub-reviewers within 5 s. Use mocks for Claude Code spawning — test orchestration logic, not LLM calls.
acceptanceCriteria: All 5 integration test scenarios pass
Mocks replace actual Claude spawning — tests run in CI without LLM API keys
Reap test uses real tmux sessions with a stub process, confirms sessions gone within 5 s
Test file ≤ 300 lines
npm test passes

items.title: Replay 3 historical PRs to validate verdict parity
items.description: Pick 3 recently merged PRs: one CHANGES_REQUESTED-then-fixed, one APPROVED on first pass, one with a known security finding. Run each through the new pipeline against the original diff. Compare new verdict to recorded ground-truth. Document in docs/review-validation-report.md. Any mismatch must be explained with policy justification. All 'clean' PRs must produce APPROVED under the new severity calibration.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 historical PRs replayed against new pipeline
Verdicts documented in docs/review-validation-report.md: old verdict, new verdict, match/mismatch
Any mismatch explained with policy justification
All clean PRs produce APPROVED under new severity calibration
The PR with a known security finding still produces CHANGES_REQUESTED

items.title: Gate new pipeline behind experimental.synthesisAsOrchestrator flag
items.description: Add experimental.synthesisAsOrchestrator boolean to dashboard Settings > Experimental (default: false). When true, routes pan review request through the new spawnRun path. Label: 'Synthesis as orchestrator (PAN-1059)'. Config persisted to projects.yaml. After 1 week of clean canary data, flip default to true, then after 2 more weeks remove the flag entirely.
acceptanceCriteria: experimental.synthesisAsOrchestrator visible in Settings > Experimental
Default false in shipped config
When true: new spawnRun path used for pan review request
Config persisted across dashboard restarts
npm run typecheck and npm test pass

items.title: Add review-role cost tracking and baseline measurement for canary
items.description: Before flipping flag default-on, confirm G2 (≥75% token reduction). Ensure cost events capture role: 'review' with sub-breakdown (review.security, review.correctness, etc.). Dashboard per-issue cost view shows review role total and sub-breakdown. Add pan cost review-baseline CLI command to compute mean review-role token spend over last N issues. Document before/after numbers in docs/review-validation-report.md.
acceptanceCriteria: Cost events record role: 'review' and sub-role breakdowns per issue
Dashboard cost view shows review role total and sub-breakdown
pan cost review-baseline outputs mean tokens/issue over last N review rounds
Baseline documented in docs/review-validation-report.md before canary starts
Post-canary measurement documented showing actual % reduction
